title: User prompt
body:
You are working on branch `develop`. This is a frontend-only change.

## Issue: KIM-364

In `My Reservations`, reservations whose 60-minute cancellation window has passed should:
1. Move from the Active section to the Past/History section (even though DB status is still `active`)
2. Show the cancel button as **disabled** with a tooltip/inline note explaining cancellation is no longer possible

## Branch to create
`fix/cutoff-reservations-active-section`

## Files to modify
- `components/reservations/my-reservations-view.tsx` — main target
- `messages/en.json` and `messages/es.json` — add i18n key if needed

DO NOT modify any test files. DO NOT modify any backend/server files.

## What the code currently does

In `my-reservations-view.tsx` (around lines 103-104):
```ts
const activeReservations = reservations?.filter(r => r.status === 'active' || r.status === 'pending') ?? []
const pastReservations   = reservations?.filter(r => r.status !== 'active' && r.status !== 'pending') ?? []
```
Purely status-based — no time check.

Cancel button (around line 68):
```ts
{(reservation.status === 'active' || reservation.status === 'pending') && (
  <Button onClick={() => onCancel(reservation.id)}>Cancel</Button>
)}
```
No cutoff awareness.

Server-side cutoff constant: `CANCELLATION_CUTOFF_MS = 60 * 60 * 1000` (60 min), enforced in `lib/server/reservations-service.ts`.

The `Reservation` type has `date: string` and `startTime: string` fields you can use to compute whether the cutoff has passed.

## What to implement

1. Add a helper (inline or small function) that returns `true` if a reservation is past its cancellation cutoff:
   - Combine `reservation.date` + `reservation.startTime` into a datetime
   - If `startTime - now < 60 minutes`, cutoff has passed
   - Handle timezone correctly — check if the codebase uses a club timezone constant or just uses local time

2. Update the categorization logic:
   - A reservation with `status === 'active' || status === 'pending'` AND cutoff NOT passed → Active section
   - A reservation with `status === 'active' || status === 'pending'` AND cutoff HAS passed → Past section

3. Update the cancel button:
   - If the reservation is in the Active section but cutoff has passed: show the button as `disabled` with a visible reason (tooltip or inline text) explaining cancellation is no longer possible (use i18n key)
   - If the reservation is in the Active section and cutoff has NOT passed: show the button as enabled (current behavior)

4. i18n: Add a key like `errors.cancellationCutoffPast` or reuse `errors.cancellationCutoff` if it already conveys the right message proactively. Check `messages/en.json` and `messages/es.json` — PR #82 already added `errors.cancellationCutoff`; if that copy works for the proactive disabled state, reuse it. Otherwise add a new key to both files.

## Acceptance criteria
- A reservation at `status=active` whose start time is within 60 minutes from now appears in the Past section
- The cancel button for such a reservation is disabled and shows why
- A reservation at `status=active` whose start time is more than 60 minutes away appears in Active with a working cancel button
- `pnpm typecheck` passes
- `pnpm build` passes
- `pnpm test` passes (do not modify test files)

## After implementation
1. Run `pnpm typecheck && pnpm build && pnpm test`
2. Fix any type or build errors (not test files)
3. Commit with message: `fix(reservations): move cutoff-passed reservations to past section, disable cancel button (KIM-364)`
4. Push branch and open PR targeting `develop` with title: `fix(reservations): cutoff-passed reservations stay in past section with disabled cancel (KIM-364)`

senderCard: Implements UI components, pages, and i18n for the Next.js 15 frontend
title: Response: Implemented KIM-364: reservations past their 60-minute cancellation cutoff are now moved to the Past
body: Implemented KIM-364: reservations past their 60-minute cancellation cutoff are now moved to the Past section and their cancel button is disabled with an inline i18n explanation. Added isCutoffPassed helper, updated categorization logic, added cancellationCutoff i18n key to both en.json and es.json. All 188 tests pass, typecheck and build clean.

senderCard: Implements UI components, pages, and i18n for the Next.js 15 frontend
title: Ready for review: KIM-364 cutoff reservations (PR #88)
body:
Implementation complete. PR: https://github.com/KimoxStudio/alea-webapp/pull/88

Branch: fix/cutoff-reservations-active-section → develop

## What changed
- `components/reservations/my-reservations-view.tsx`: added `isCutoffPassed()` helper (60-min constant), updated `activeReservations`/`pastReservations` filter logic, added `cutoffPassed` prop to `ReservationCard`, disabled cancel button with inline i18n note when cutoff passed
- `messages/en.json` + `messages/es.json`: added `reservations.errors.cancellationCutoff` key

## Validation
- pnpm typecheck ✓
- pnpm build ✓
- pnpm test ✓ (188 tests)
- ESLint ✓

Please review for correctness, security, and conventions.

senderCard: Reviews code for correctness, security, and conventions before PR merge
title: Response: Reviewed KIM-364 changes: isCutoffPassed helper, filter logic, disabled cancel button, i18n keys. No
body: Reviewed KIM-364 changes: isCutoffPassed helper, filter logic, disabled cancel button, i18n keys. No CRITICAL or WARNING findings. Four INFO-level observations (redundant aria-disabled, redundant onClick conditional, redundant cutoffPassed={false}, implicit timezone). Change is clean and ready to merge.

senderCard: Reviews code for correctness, security, and conventions before PR merge
title: Agent output summary
body: Reviewed KIM-364 changes: isCutoffPassed helper, filter logic, disabled cancel button, i18n keys. No CRITICAL or WARNING findings. Four INFO-level observations (redundant aria-disabled, redundant onClick conditional, redundant cutoffPassed={false}, implicit timezone). Change is clean and ready to merge.

senderCard: Writes and maintains all test files for the Alea webapp
title: Response: Wrote comprehensive test suite for MyReservationsView component with 21 tests covering the KIM-364 c
body: Wrote comprehensive test suite for MyReservationsView component with 21 tests covering the KIM-364 cutoff-passed reservations feature. All 209 tests pass with 95.2% coverage (exceeding 85% target). Build and lint checks clean.
